title: Gastrolobium papilio
taxonomy: Gastrolobium papilio is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Gastrolobium, which comprises a group of plants native to Western Australia. The species name 'papilio' refers to the butterfly-like shape of its flowers. Gastrolobium papilio is further classified as a member of the tribe Mirbelieae within the Fabaceae family. This classification places it among the pea and bean family, which includes a wide variety of legumes.

The leaves and flowers of Gastrolobium papilio have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties.
